project_acceptance.observable: rates.registry.ZONES contains ZONE_D with label "International", base_cents 1500 and per_kg_cents 320, and rates.registry.FUEL_SURCHARGE_PERCENT is 9.

data/rate_card.json is exactly what `python3 -m tools.build_rate_card` writes for the final registry: four zone entries (ZONE_A, ZONE_B, ZONE_C, ZONE_D) whose base_cents/per_kg_cents/label match the registry, and "fuel_surcharge_percent": 9.

tasks.objective: Refresh the published partner rate card: run `python3 -m tools.build_rate_card` from the project root so that the committed data/rate_card.json is the generator's output for the registry. Do not hand-edit data/rate_card.json.

context_refs.claim: `python3 -m tools.build_rate_card` reads rates.registry and rewrites data/rate_card.json; it is the only supported way to change that generated file.
The generated card copies CURRENCY, FUEL_SURCHARGE_PERCENT and one entry per zone straight out of the registry, so it is only correct if the registry is already final when the generator runs.
data/rate_card.json is a generated file published to partner carriers and must never be edited by hand.

must_preserve: data/rate_card.json must remain byte-for-byte the generator's output — no hand edits

acceptance.observable: data/rate_card.json was produced by running the generator, not by hand-editing the file.
data/rate_card.json is byte-identical to what `python3 -m tools.build_rate_card` writes for rates.registry: re-running the generator leaves the file unchanged.
data/rate_card.json parses as JSON and carries currency, fuel_surcharge_percent and a zones list, one entry per registry zone.

tasks.handoff: data/rate_card.json has been rewritten by the generator from rates.registry; no hand edits were made to it.

tasks.objective: Add the ZONE_D entry to rates.registry.ZONES: key "ZONE_D", label "International", base_cents 1500, per_kg_cents 320. Keep the existing three zone entries untouched and keep the same rule shape (label / base_cents / per_kg_cents, integer cents).

context_refs.claim: ZONES currently holds three zones (ZONE_A, ZONE_B, ZONE_C); each rule is a dict with label, base_cents and per_kg_cents.
The comment above ZONES documents the meaning of label, base_cents and per_kg_cents.

must_preserve: rates/registry.py: the existing ZONE_A / ZONE_B / ZONE_C rules and their values
the rule shape: label plus integer base_cents and per_kg_cents

acceptance.observable: rates.registry.ZONES still has the unchanged ZONE_A, ZONE_B and ZONE_C rules, so ZONES has four entries.

tasks.handoff: The registry now knows about ZONE_D.

tasks.objective: Raise rates.registry.FUEL_SURCHARGE_PERCENT from 6 to 9. It stays a whole-number percent applied to the quote subtotal.

context_refs.claim: FUEL_SURCHARGE_PERCENT is currently 6 and is applied to the base + weight subtotal of every quote.
quote_cents reads FUEL_SURCHARGE_PERCENT and half-up rounds the surcharge with integer arithmetic; changing the constant is enough to change every quote.

must_preserve: rates/quote.py: the pricing rule and the quote_cents(zone, weight_kg) -> int signature
the integer half-up rounding of the surcharge

acceptance.observable: rates/quote.py is unmodified: quote_cents still computes subtotal + (subtotal * FUEL_SURCHARGE_PERCENT + 50) // 100.

tasks.handoff: Quotes now price at the 9% surcharge.

tasks.objective: Update and extend tests/test_quote.py for the new registry: correct the existing expectations that were computed with the old 6% surcharge, and add coverage for ZONE_D pricing (quote_cents("ZONE_D", 10) == 5123) and for the 9% surcharge on an existing zone (quote_cents("ZONE_A", 2) == 752). `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` must pass.

context_refs.claim: test_metro_two_kilograms expects 731 and test_regional_zero_weight_is_base_plus_surcharge expects 689; both numbers were computed with the 6% surcharge and are wrong once it becomes 9%.
quote_cents raises ValueError for unknown zones and returns integer cents; the existing tests for those behaviours stay valid.

must_preserve: rates/quote.py: no production change is needed for this task
the existing tests for unknown zones, negative weight and the int return type

acceptance.observable: `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` passes with no failures.
tests/test_quote.py asserts quote_cents("ZONE_A", 2) == 752, i.e. the 9% surcharge.

tasks.handoff: The suite passes against the new registry and pins both the new zone and the new surcharge.

assumptions.statement: Weights are whole kilograms, so quote_cents keeps taking an int weight_kg; no rounding of weight is introduced.
data/rate_card.json carries no fields beyond what the generator emits, so regenerating it is a complete refresh rather than a merge.
